pattern: Splice two already-sorted lists into one by repeatedly attaching whichever current head is smaller, then linking the leftover tail.
hints: Both inputs are already sorted, so you never need to look more than one node ahead in each list to decide what comes next.
Use a dummy node as a stand-in head; keep a tail pointer and at each step append the smaller of the two current nodes, advancing only that list.
When one list runs out, the other is already sorted, so attach the remaining list wholesale to the tail and return dummy.next.

edge_inputs.description: interleaving with duplicate values across lists
all of one list is smaller than all of the other (no interleave)
one list much longer than the other

stress.description: two long random sorted lists at the length cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = rng.randint(0, min(size, 50))
    n2 = rng.randint(0, min(size, 50))
    a = sorted(rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n1))
    b = sorted(rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n2))
    return [a, b]
stress.note: each list independently sorted within constraints
